description: Fires a tag on a selected data layer event on all pages. By default it fires for the selected event on any page; you can add conditions so the tag fires only for a specific page or under specific conditions. Use it to react to business events like form submission, purchase, signup, add to cart, etc.

data_layer_event_condition.description: Fires the tag when the selected event occurs in the data layer (e.g. when a visitor adds a product to their cart). This condition is always required.

builtin_variable.name: Event
builtin_variable.description: A variable used in the data layer event trigger. It reads the event name from the data layer.
builtin_variable.usage: Use this variable_id in the condition that defines when the trigger fires. Set the condition value to the data layer event name specified by the user (e.g. purchase_completed, lead_created, signup_completed).
builtin_variable.value_description: Data layer event name specified by the user. E.g. addToCart, purchase_completed, lead_created, signup_completed.

event_trigger_conditions_reference.note: The Event condition is always required: use the built-in variable 'Event' (id 977a1f9d-4fdb-43bf-9549-bdf68a7b69a6) and set value to the data layer event name (e.g. addToCart, purchase_completed). You can add other conditions (page, referrer, campaign, etc.) to fire only on specific pages or under specific conditions.

event_conditions_reference.description: Optional conditions to fire the tag only when the selected event occurs on a specific page or under specific conditions.

condition_types.description: Fires when the visitor is on a page with the selected page URL and the selected event occurs in the data layer.
Fires when the visitor is on a page with the selected path and the selected event occurs in the data layer.
Fires when the visitor is on a page hosted on the selected domain and the selected event occurs in the data layer.
Fires when the visitor was directed by the selected internal referrer and the selected event occurs in the data layer.
Fires when the visitor was directed by the selected external referrer and the selected event occurs in the data layer.
Fires when the visitor was directed by the selected campaign (utm_campaign) and the selected event occurs in the data layer.
Fires when the visitor was directed by the selected traffic source and the selected event occurs. Values: campaign, organic search, social, referral, direct.
Fires when the visitor is a returning visitor (stg_returning_visitor cookie) and the selected event occurs in the data layer.
Any variable you add can be used to define trigger conditions.

when_to_use: You push application events into the data layer and want to trigger tags on those events
You need to track granular business actions (e.g., purchase, signup, lead_created)
You want to migrate from GTM custom events to Piwik PRO
common_use_cases: custom_event from SPA routers or internal app events
best_practices: Use consistent event naming conventions (snake_case or kebab-case)
Document expected event payload structure for your team
Create dedicated variables to extract event name/properties from the data layer
Test events in the browser console before publishing

create_trigger.description: Create a new event trigger using this template.

conditions.description: Array of condition objects. Must include at least one condition with variable_id 977a1f9d-4fdb-43bf-9549-bdf68a7b69a6 (Event), value = data layer event name (e.g. purchase_completed). May include conditions on event payload or page.

update_trigger.description: Update existing event trigger - only editable fields will be processed

conditions.description: Array of condition objects. Must include at least one condition using the built-in Event variable (variable_id 977a1f9d-4fdb-43bf-9549-bdf68a7b69a6) with value set to the data layer event name (e.g. purchase_completed, lead_created). May include additional conditions on event payload or page context.

variable_id.for_event_name: Use built-in Event variable_id 977a1f9d-4fdb-43bf-9549-bdf68a7b69a6; value = data layer event name specified by user

common_variables.event_name: Data layer variable pointing to event name (built-in Event 977a1f9d-4fdb-43bf-9549-bdf68a7b69a6)
common_variables.event_property: Data layer variable pointing to a payload field (e.g. ecommerce.action)

conditions.recommendation: Include one condition with variable_id 977a1f9d-4fdb-43bf-9549-bdf68a7b69a6 (Event), condition_type e.g. equals, value = the data layer event name; add conditions on payload or page as needed.

event_name_equals.description: Fire on a specific event name
event_name_equals.variable_type: Data Layer variable for event name (e.g., 'event')

event_name_pattern.description: Fire on events following a naming pattern
event_name_pattern.variable_type: Data Layer variable for event name

event_property_check.description: Fire based on event payload property
event_property_check.variable_type: Data Layer variable for payload field (e.g., 'ecommerce.action')

lead_created_event_with_property.description: Trigger on lead_created with channel property set to 'paid'

add_to_cart_event.description: Trigger on 'addToCart' data layer event (per help example)

common_mistakes: Omitting the Event condition (variable_id 977a1f9d-4fdb-43bf-9549-bdf68a7b69a6 with value = data layer event name)
Using an event name variable that doesn't reflect your data layer structure
Relying on properties that are not always present in the payload
Removing or reusing condition_id values incorrectly when editing existing conditions

possible_causes: Missing or wrong Event condition (use variable_id 977a1f9d-4fdb-43bf-9549-bdf68a7b69a6, value = data layer event name)
Data layer push occurs after trigger evaluation or not on the expected page
Variable IDs point to the wrong data layer paths
debugging_steps: Ensure one condition uses variable_id 977a1f9d-4fdb-43bf-9549-bdf68a7b69a6 (Event), value = the data layer event name (e.g. purchase_completed)
Verify data layer pushes in browser console (window.dataLayer or window._mtm)
Log pushed events and payload structure to confirm fields
Test conditions with simpler 'equals' or 'contains' first
Ensure the variable returns the expected value

event_name_variable.description: Variable that reads the event name (e.g., path 'event') from the data layer

event_property_variable.description: Variable that reads a payload property (e.g., 'ecommerce.action')
event_property_variable.best_for: Refining event triggers by payload attributes
